title: Creamy Chilli Venezuelan Chicken Bowl
description: A flavorful and spicy Venezuelan dish featuring chicken, birds-eye chillies, and a creamy sauce, served in an earthenware bowl. This recipe is Buddhist-temple-friendly and can be made with leaf-wrap-steam method.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the chopped birds-eye chillies, mustard, and coconut cream to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the chopped cilantro, lime juice, salt, and black pepper.
Serve the chicken and sauce over rice or with tortillas, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
To make the leaf-wrap-steam method, place a portion of the chicken and sauce in the center of a large leaf, such as a banana leaf, and fold the leaf over the filling to form a packet.
Steam the packets over boiling water for 5-10 minutes, until the filling is hot and the leaf is tender.
